task_completion.description: Create the R script file at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open file named 'garch_analysis.R' in a text editor or IDE, or terminal commands creating/editing this file at '/home/ga/RProjects/garch_analysis.R'.

task_completion.description: Read the SPY historical price data and calculate log-returns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for R code in the script that reads from '/home/ga/RProjects/datasets/spy_prices.csv' (e.g., using read.csv) and computes log-returns (e.g., using diff(log(...)) or Return.calculate).

task_completion.description: Specify and fit the GARCH(1,1) model using the rugarch package.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'rugarch' package being loaded (library(rugarch)) and a model specification using 'ugarchspec' with variance.model = list(model = 'sGARCH', garchOrder = c(1, 1)), mean.model = list(armaOrder = c(1, 0)), and distribution.model = 'norm'.

task_completion.description: Calculate 1-day VaR estimates at 95% and 99% confidence levels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for R code that extracts or calculates the 95% (0.95) and 99% (0.99) Value-at-Risk (VaR) from the fitted GARCH model (e.g., using the 'quantile' function on the rugarch fit object).

task_completion.description: Output the VaR estimates to the specified output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for R code (like write.csv) saving the calculated VaR estimates to a path starting with '/home/ga/RProjects/output/spy' (e.g., spy_var_estimates.csv).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent dynamically calculates the VaR values rather than hardcoding them.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the R script to ensure VaR values are derived from the model fit object and not typed out as static numeric arrays.

integrity.description: Ensure the file contains valid R code and not just pseudocode or plain text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the contents of 'garch_analysis.R' follow R syntax (e.g., <- assignment operators, valid function calls).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually runs the R script to generate the outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output showing the execution of the script (e.g., 'Rscript garch_analysis.R') or the presence of the generated output files in the file explorer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires an R script at /home/ga/RProjects/garch_analysis.R. It must read from /home/ga/RProjects/datasets/spy_prices.csv. The model must be specified using the 'rugarch' package: sGARCH(1,1) variance, ARMA(1,0) mean, and 'norm' distribution. It must output 1-day VaR estimates at 95% and 99% confidence levels to files starting with /home/ga/RProjects/output/spy (specifically spy_var_estimates.csv, and potentially spy_backtest.csv / spy_garch_report.png).
